name: Vanta
blurb: Category-leading compliance automation with continuous monitoring and strong SOC 2 muscle; CMMC coverage shipped 2024, but the focus is SaaS multi-framework rather than DIB-native readiness.

name: Drata
blurb: Close number two to Vanta with a stronger FedRAMP story and growing CMMC coverage; SaaS-first by DNA.

name: Secureframe
blurb: Automation-heavy compliance platform with a strong AI story; partners with C3PAO RSM for streamlined CMMC assessments.

name: Sprinto
blurb: Lower-priced global compliance automation for audit readiness and continuous compliance; CMMC is not a stated focus.

name: Thoropass
blurb: Bundles audit and automation (formerly Laika) with managed audit readiness; CMMC is not a primary lane.

name: Anecdotes
blurb: Evidence-graph, agentic compliance platform for complex multi-framework programs and continuous posture; CMMC is not native.

name: Scrut Automation
blurb: Aggressively priced global compliance automation; CMMC support announced in 2024, with DIB-specific depth still building.

name: TrustCloud
blurb: Freemium compliance automation disrupting the price floor; CMMC fit is limited.

name: Apptega
blurb: Channel-first (MSP/MSSP) compliance management with decent CMMC coverage.

name: Hyperproof
blurb: Workflow-heavy GRC with stronger NIST/CMMC alignment than the SaaS-first platforms; a better fit for orgs already past 0 to 1.

name: ServiceNow GRC
blurb: Enterprise GRC heavyweight with a powerful workflow engine and heavy implementation lift; not targeted at SMBs.

name: Archer
blurb: Legacy enterprise GRC (RSA Archer) with strong risk management and a slower pace of innovation.

name: MetricStream
blurb: Long-standing enterprise IRM with broad coverage; not federal or DIB-differentiated.

name: OneTrust
blurb: Came from privacy and expanded into GRC with strong data mapping; CMMC is not a core lane.

name: LogicGate
blurb: Modern, configurable no-code IRM; more flexible than Archer and enterprise-priced.

name: AuditBoard
blurb: Audit-led platform with SOX origins expanding into GRC; CMMC is not a stated focus.

name: Workiva
blurb: Financial-reporting and SOX heavyweight for public companies; CMMC is out of scope.

name: Diligent
blurb: Board-governance-led GRC (Galvanize/HighBond) with an enterprise sales motion.

name: IBM OpenPages
blurb: Legacy enterprise GRC strong in banking and regulated industries; limited DIB footprint.

name: SAP GRC
blurb: GRC and access controls bolted onto SAP installs; DIB SMB is not a stated segment.

name: FutureFeed
blurb: CMMC-native tool strong on 110-control mapping and SPRS score tracking; lighter on broader GRC and continuous monitoring.

name: ComplianceForge
blurb: Policy and document templates built on the Secure Controls Framework; document-first rather than SaaS, and heavily used by RPOs.

name: Totem
blurb: DIB-focused, SMB-friendly CMMC tool with a strong template library; lighter on continuous monitoring.

name: Cyturus
blurb: CMMC and risk tool built around an Adaptive Risk Model; stronger on quantification than peers.

name: Exostar
blurb: DIB identity and supply-chain federation mandated by some primes; functions as quasi-infrastructure for the defense supply chain.

name: Virtru
blurb: Email and data encryption with a strong DIB footprint for protecting CUI.

name: PreVeil
blurb: End-to-end encrypted email and files for CUI; a strong DIB SMB fit with a lighter lift than GCC High.

name: Microsoft GCC High
blurb: Microsoft Government Community Cloud High; a de facto requirement for many DIB contractors handling CUI.

name: Proofpoint
blurb: Email-security incumbent; CMMC-adjacent via DLP and email controls.

name: Duo Security
blurb: Cisco-owned MFA; a practical building block for the CMMC access-control and identification families.

name: BitSight
blurb: Security-ratings leader used by primes to assess subs; outside-in only.

name: SecurityScorecard
blurb: Direct BitSight competitor on the same outside-in ratings model; expanding into questionnaire automation.

name: UpGuard
blurb: Combines third-party risk with attack-surface management; mid-market friendly.

name: Panorays
blurb: Questionnaire automation for third-party risk; workflow-heavy.

name: Prevalent
blurb: Program-led, enterprise-focused third-party risk management.

name: TrustArc
blurb: Privacy-program-management heritage; adjacent to but outside the CMMC stack.

name: Securiti
blurb: Privacy and data-security convergence; enterprise-focused.

name: BigID
blurb: Data discovery and classification; CMMC-adjacent via CUI identification.

name: DataGrail
blurb: Subject-rights and DSAR automation; privacy-focused, with CMMC out of scope.

name: Osano
blurb: Affordable mid-market privacy (cookie, consent, DSAR) with a narrow scope.

name: Centraleyes
blurb: Integrated, AI-powered GRC that maps controls across common frameworks with built-in third-party risk workflows.

name: CISO Assistant
blurb: The leading open-source GRC platform (by intuitem) covering risk, compliance, audit, and third-party risk across 150+ frameworks, with a free community edition and a paid Pro tier.

name: eramba
blurb: Open-source GRC platform for risk, controls, policies, and audits, with paid enterprise support.

name: SimpleRisk
blurb: Affordable, fast-to-deploy GRC for governance, risk, and compliance, including vendor assessments; offers an open-source core.

name: Risk Cognizance
blurb: AI-powered GRC spanning ERM, audit, policy, and third-party risk; multi-framework including CMMC, and sold to enterprises and MSSPs.

name: IntelliGRC
blurb: GRC tool purpose-built for CMMC and compliance automation for defense contractors.

name: Greypike
blurb: End-to-end CMMC compliance with a certified-enclave and scope-reduction approach plus managed compliance.

name: Oneleet
blurb: All-in-one, security-first compliance combining automation, security testing, and auditor partnerships.

name: Optro
blurb: Agentic, AI-powered GRC for audit, cyber risk, compliance, and AI governance, built on a unified data core.

name: Paramify
blurb: Compliance automation that generates federal packages (such as the SSP) and collects evidence for FedRAMP, FISMA, and CMMC.

name: SmartSuite
blurb: Work-OS platform with a Connected GRC solution spanning risk, compliance, audit, third-party risk, and privacy.

name: ZenGRC
blurb: GRC platform (ZenGRC by Reciprocity, formerly ROAR) for risk and compliance management, now with agentic-AI features; entry tier starts around $2.5k/mo.
